frontier: Erdős #979 / OEIS A385316: what is a(6), the smallest integer expressible as a sum of three prime cubes in exactly 6 ways?
action:
Milestone: bounded_exact_search; contract=sha256:5c3f895292d59b11fc8f37a0f70c223f24ee5aa5a6d73b5948bfd5c1e9c4c69b
Authored a self-contained Python verifier (prime_cubes_verifier.py) that builds the complete prime set P={p : p³≤C}, enumerates all nondecreasing triples (p≤q≤r) with p³+q³+r³≤C, and counts exact representation multiplicities. Ran verified replay at C=10⁹, C=10¹⁰, and C=10¹¹. All five OEIS terms a(1)..a(5) reproduced exactly at C=10¹¹. Kill fixtures (2 domain, 2 predicate) all rejected correctly.
verified:
Domain kill fixtures: 2/2 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 2/2 rejected correctly
Artifact: prime_cubes_verifier.py, SHA-256: f53a78ebc4b69b9d3c02628cefbb3fcfe967a09796ee476e9a7017f3dc133150
Replay at C=10¹¹: known terms match a(1)=24, a(2)=185527, a(3)=8627527, a(4)=999979163, a(5)=10588881419. k-distribution {1:31346765, 2:126221, 3:1080, 4:14, 5:2}, zero entries at k=6.
result: Verified the exact-prime-set bounded search methodology; independently confirmed certified bound a(6) > 10¹¹. The discriminating step C=10¹³ requires DGX SSH (spark-38e3.local) and cannot be reached from this Mac. No k=6 candidate exists below 10¹¹.
next_gate: An operator with DGX SSH access must execute the same search at C=10¹³. If a k=6 candidate appears, report it; if not, advance bound to a(6) > 10¹³ and escalate to C=10¹⁴ or a heuristic/construction approach.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training.
